name: Raccoon

metaTitle: Raccoon Facts | Habitat, Diet, Paws & Photos
metaDescription: Discover raccoon facts with photos, forest habitat, omnivore diet, kits, and night behavior. Learn where raccoons live and why they wash their food.
searchIntents: what is a baby raccoon called
why do raccoons wash their food

lifespan.captivity: Up to 20 years in human care

funFacts: Raccoons are most active at night.
Their front paws can open jars, latches, and trash cans.
Raccoons often dip food in water before eating.
They can climb trees and swim well.
Raccoons have spread to Europe and Japan after being introduced.

faq.question: What is a raccoon?
faq.answer: A raccoon is a medium-sized mammal with a black mask, ringed tail, and clever front paws. The common raccoon lives across North America.

faq.question: Where do raccoons live?
faq.answer: Raccoons live in forests, wetlands, and cities across North America. They have also been introduced to parts of Europe and Asia.

faq.question: What do raccoons eat?
faq.answer: Raccoons eat fruit, nuts, insects, fish, eggs, and many foods they find near people, including trash.

faq.question: What is a baby raccoon called?
faq.answer: A baby raccoon is called a kit. Kits are born in spring and stay with their mother for months.

faq.question: Why do raccoons wash their food?
faq.answer: Raccoons often dip food in water, which may help them feel their food better with their sensitive paws.

faq.question: Are raccoons nocturnal?
faq.answer: Yes. Raccoons usually come out at night to search for food and avoid predators.

faq.question: Are raccoons dangerous?
faq.answer: Wild raccoons should not be approached. They can bite if scared and may carry diseases.
